pi_items.verified_value: BBBC005 from Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection at https://data.broadinstitute.org/bbbc/BBBC005/
CSV path consistent with export_result.sh and task description

pi_items.source: Searched BBBC005 official page, Lehmussola et al. SIMCEP papers, BBBC ground truth files, and actual downloaded TIFF metadata. BBBC005 official documentation (https://bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005) does not specify pixel scale. Downloaded images (696x520 pixels) contain TIFF metadata with DPI=(72,72) but this is printing DPI, not microscopy scale.

pi_items.verified_value: 15% tolerance used in verifier.py line 77 for measurement accuracy evaluation
pi_items.source: verifier.py analysis - measurement accuracy is checked as: if percent_error <= 15, full 40 pts awarded

pi_items.verified_value: BBBC005 - Synthetic cells generated via SIMCEP simulator, 696x520 pixels, 9600 simulated microscopy images of cell body stained cells
Confirmed - BBBC005 contains synthetically generated cell images created using the SIMCEP simulation platform, not real microscopy images
pi_items.source: setup_fiji.sh comments state 'BBBC005 - Synthetic cells for testing segmentation' and BBBC website describes as simulated images

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly in metadata, but code assumes 696x520
pi_items.verified_value: 696x520 pixels confirmed by direct inspection of BBBC005_v1_images.zip images using PIL
pi_items.source: Downloaded and opened BBBC005_v1_images/SIMCEPImages_A14_C57_F1_s21_w1.TIF - PIL confirms Size: (696, 520), Mode: L (8-bit grayscale)

pi_items.source: BBBC005 zip file listing and PIL metadata inspection

privileged_info_summary: BBBC005 is a real, publicly available synthetic microscopy dataset from the Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection containing 9,600 simulated cell images (696x520 pixels, 8-bit grayscale TIF format) generated using the SIMCEP simulator. Images come in w1 (cell body) and w2 (nucleus) channel pairs. The task measures cell polarity by calculating distance between cell body centroid and nucleus centroid with a 15% measurement tolerance. However, the stated pixel scale of 0.65 µm/pixel is not documented in official BBBC005 sources or TIFF metadata, and could not be independently verified.
